name: QwenPaw System Architecture
description: Intent architecture for QwenPaw covering the current platform slices plus the strengthened P0 local security audit foundation required for end-to-end non-repudiation, tamper-evident auditing, external checkpoint anchoring, out-of-band configuration tamper detection, tiered audit durability, replay-resistant cloud reconciliation, and behavior-level anomaly detection. The security extension is scoped from the AgentScope security infrastructure PRD and is intentionally limited to the minimum business closure needed for four core business scenarios and three deep-risk scenarios: full-chain evidence continuity, audit-integrity self-healing lock mode, bounded TTFT impact under full audit mode, lease-expiry-driven active defense, prompt-injection-resistant tool enforcement, stolen-session replay blocking, and silent exfiltration pattern alerting.

attributes.name: evidence_policy
attributes.value: repository-current-state-plus-prd-scoped-security-foundation

elements.name: QwenPaw Platform
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform
elements.description:
Repository-backed personal AI assistant platform spanning backend runtime, local operator CLI, operator console, documentation website, architecture validation assets, and the P0 security audit foundation required for local safe operation.
#file:README.md
#file:pyproject.toml
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Backend Runtime
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime
elements.description:
Python runtime root that owns API routers, agent execution, channels, config, security, providers, and shared utilities.
#file:src/qwenpaw
#file:tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py
#file:tests/unit/routers/test_git.py

elements.name: Local Security Audit Foundation
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation
elements.description:
Backend-owned P0 security foundation that standardizes implicit SecurityContext propagation, defines the canonical AuditEvent envelope, persists append-only local audit records with a hash chain, anchors continuity through a restricted checkpoint store, verifies startup consistency against configuration hashes, enforces security lock mode after integrity failures, supports human-in-the-loop confirmation for high-risk tool execution, resists prompt-driven guard bypass at the tool-call layer, and supports tiered audit durability for critical versus informational events. This element is intentionally limited to the minimum closure needed for end-to-end non-repudiation evidence chains, audit-integrity self-healing lock mode, bounded TTFT impact, active liveness defense, replay-resistant session continuity, and behavior-anomaly evidence generation rather than the full later audit pipeline.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/auth.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/agent_context.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/runner/utils.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/routers/config.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/routers/settings.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/security

attributes.name: verification_focus
attributes.value: end-to-end actor trace continuity, tamper-evident and self-healing audit integrity, accountable security configuration changes, prompt-injection-resistant high-risk enforcement, replay-resistant session continuity, behavior-level anomaly surfacing, and bounded full-audit latency

attributes.name: scope_limit
attributes.value: P0 covers implicit context propagation via contextvars, local append-only audit write, checkpoint-anchored hash continuity, configuration-hash consistency verification, security lock mode, basic query, approval persistence, human-in-the-loop confirmation records for high-risk tool execution, tool-layer enforcement against prompt-driven bypass attempts, tiered L1/L2 audit write semantics, asynchronous uplink of critical audit anchors to the cloud situational awareness platform for remote integrity mirroring, and cloud-visible anomaly evidence for replay or silent exfiltration patterns; session encryption remains later phases.

attributes.name: context_propagation_rule
attributes.value: SecurityContext must not be architecture-visible as an explicit function parameter; request entry and message dispatch must rely on implicit contextvars capture and restoration.

attributes.name: checkpoint_rule
attributes.value: A restricted append-only checkpoint store must anchor latest anchored_event_id and global_hash every N events and on each high-risk configuration change so tail truncation is detectable on restart.

attributes.name: lock_mode_rule
attributes.value: If startup consistency verification detects settings or audit-anchor divergence, backend runtime enters security lock mode and refuses sensitive tool loading until trust is re-established.

attributes.name: ledger_integrity_verification_rule
attributes.value: Audit integrity verification must cover the committed audit ledger, not only checkpoint-file presence. Editing, deleting, reordering, or truncating any already-written audit record, including a non-tail record after several high-risk actions, is a tamper signal that must be detected no later than the next high-risk tool boundary or startup/reconnect preflight.

attributes.name: security_center_recovery_display_rule
attributes.value: After audit-ledger tamper is detected, Security Center backend and operator web must keep the affected canonical client in UNTRUSTED or equivalent recovery-required state until full-chain validation against the cloud mirror succeeds; a CLEAR display before that validation is a security failure.

attributes.name: audit_tiering_rule
attributes.value: L1 critical events use synchronous durable write plus hash-chain and checkpoint updates before returning; L2 informational reasoning events may batch asynchronously and remain visible as pending verification until anchored.

attributes.name: uplink_rule
attributes.value: Critical audit anchors and L1 audit records must be asynchronously uplinked with retry to a cloud integrity mirror; before sending new data after reconnect, the client must perform a pre-upload hash-alignment handshake and refuse sync if local and cloud anchors diverge.

attributes.name: high_risk_confirmation_rule
attributes.value: High-risk tool execution must pause for explicit user confirmation, persist the confirmation artifact, and emit a synchronous L1 USER_CONFIRMATION audit record before execution resumes.

attributes.name: tool_layer_enforcement_rule
attributes.value: High-risk enforcement must execute at the tool invocation boundary rather than trusting conversational intent classification; prompts that claim test mode, maintenance mode, or other bypass narratives cannot suppress guard evaluation when the tool risk remains High.

attributes.name: session_replay_rule
attributes.value: Cloud-side reconciliation must validate that each trusted trace or session advances through the expected monotonic sequence and hash continuity. A replayed or cloned session presented from an unaudited process must be blocked before additional actions are authorized.

attributes.name: trust_recovery_rule
attributes.value: Whenever a client resumes from UNTRUSTED state, it must perform a mandatory full-chain sync for the missing sequence gap. No new high-risk tools are permitted until the gap is validated by the cloud mirror.

attributes.name: recovery_release_rule
attributes.value: For a lease-expired or otherwise UNTRUSTED canonical client, successful full-chain missing-gap validation by the cloud mirror must close the recovery gate and restore normal model access for that same client. Continuing to project UNTRUSTED or return recovery-gated model-access denial after valid proof is a security availability failure; restoring access before valid proof remains a security integrity failure.

attributes.name: normal_offline_reconnect_rule
attributes.value: A normally stopped or briefly offline canonical client whose local audit head still matches the cloud shadow, has no tamper evidence, has no missing sequence, and has not crossed the lease-expiry recovery boundary must be admitted as normal continuity on reconnect. Security Center backend and operator web must project ALIGNED or TRUSTED with gap_status CLEAR, recovery_gate_status CLEAR, and recovery_required false. If the lease did expire, the client may require full-chain missing-gap validation, but once a valid locally constructible proof is accepted the state must clear; a persistent GAP_VALIDATION_REQUIRED/REQUIRED/OPEN display after normal proof acceptance is an availability failure.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-024-end-to-end-non-repudiation-evidence-chain
testcases.description: When an employee triggers a high-risk action through a third-party agent plugin, the system must preserve a complete business-visible evidence chain from the original user to the final high-risk tool effect and bind that chain to the user's confirmation artifact.
testcases.Input: Authenticate as user A, ask QwenPaw to delegate a high-risk operation through Agent B and third-party plugin C toward high-risk tool D, then provide the required user confirmation so the action is allowed to continue.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center and the audit query surface expose a continuous evidence chain from user A to Agent B to plugin C to tool D together with the confirmation artifact digest, and no link in the chain is anonymous, broken, or attributed to a fallback system identity.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-025-audit-integrity-self-healing-lockdown
testcases.description: When an employee attempts to hide a sensitive action by deleting or editing audit records, editing configuration files, or reconnecting after offline tampering, the system must surface a single business-level data continuity anomaly and refuse further sensitive use until trust is re-established.
testcases.Input: Execute several sensitive actions so the local audit ledger has a multi-record baseline and Security Center has a trusted cloud anchor, then modify or truncate any committed local audit record, checkpoint, or settings database by operating-system means, including editing a non-tail audit record such as the second record. Without manually restoring trust, attempt to reconnect or invoke another sensitive tool.
testcases.TestResults: Before the device can continue sensitive work or upload new anchors, the system detects the audit-ledger divergence, raises a data continuity anomaly in Security Center, enters local UNTRUSTED lock mode, refuses sensitive tools at the high-risk boundary, and keeps Security Center backend and operator web recovery-required rather than CLEAR until the local ledger, checkpoint anchors, and cloud mirror are reconciled.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-027-lease-expiry-active-defense
testcases.description: When a QwenPaw device goes missing or stays offline beyond its lease window, Security Center must actively downgrade the device to untrusted and keep it blocked until it proves continuity on return.
testcases.Input: Let a client miss its heartbeat long enough to exceed the lease TTL, observe the cloud dashboard state turn untrusted, then reconnect the device and attempt to resume model access without first completing the missing-gap verification.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center marks the device untrusted when the lease expires, denies restored authorization on reconnect until the full missing hash gap is validated, then closes recovery_required for the same canonical client, projects ALIGNED or TRUSTED, and allows normal model access to return. Keeping the client UNTRUSTED or returning a recovery-gated denial after valid missing-gap proof fails this acceptance boundary just as much as restoring access before proof.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-028-normal-offline-reconnect-clear-state
testcases.description: When a trusted QwenPaw runtime is normally stopped or briefly offline without audit tamper, missing sequence, clone/replay evidence, or lease-expiry recovery debt, reconnecting the same canonical client must not be displayed as a missing-gap recovery incident.
testcases.Input: Start from a trusted canonical client with Security Center showing normal continuity, stop or disconnect the runtime through a normal offline path without mutating local audit evidence, then restart the same canonical client and attempt ordinary model access.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center backend and operator web show the same canonical client as ALIGNED or TRUSTED with gap_status CLEAR, recovery_gate_status CLEAR, and recovery_required false after reconnect; ordinary model access returns 200. The client must not remain in GAP_VALIDATION_REQUIRED, gap_status REQUIRED, recovery_gate_status OPEN, or a 'gap pending validation' operator display unless a real lease-expiry, tamper, missing sequence, hash divergence, clone, or replay condition has been observed.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-021-prompt-injection-tool-guard-enforced
testcases.description: A malicious prompt or contaminated external text must not be able to talk an agent into skipping the high-risk confirmation path or into treating a high-risk action as if it were a trusted test operation.
testcases.Input: In a live conversation, submit nested or deceptive instructions that claim the agent is in test mode or otherwise exempt from approval, then drive the agent toward a tool whose configured risk level is High without providing a valid trusted context or confirmation artifact.
testcases.TestResults: The high-risk tool remains blocked at the tool-call layer, the guard rejects execution because the required trusted context and confirmation are absent, and the attempted bypass is recorded as a rejected security-relevant event rather than silently executing.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-029-builtin-rule-line-ending-invariant
testcases.description: Built-in dangerous shell command rules must pass integrity verification when only line endings differ between LF and CRLF representations of the same semantic content.
testcases.Input: Stage the official signed manifest bundle with LF-canonical and CRLF-only transformations of the same dangerous_shell_commands.yaml content, then run verify_builtin_rule_files against a semantic tamper that removes a required rule without updating the manifest.
testcases.TestResults: LF-canonical and CRLF-only branches return ok=true with status ok; semantic tamper returns ok=false with status tampered and sha256_mismatch; Windows checkout must not false-positive tamper when content is unchanged.

elements.name: Implicit Security Context Manager
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Implicit Security Context Manager
elements.description:
Runtime manager that captures SecurityContext in FastAPI middleware, stores it in Python contextvars, packages it into AgentScope message metadata when crossing asynchronous message boundaries, and restores it on the consumer side so lower-layer tools can resolve identity implicitly. Explicit SecurityContext parameter threading is forbidden by this intent element.
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/auth.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/agent_context.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/runner/utils.py

elements.name: Security Context
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Security Context
elements.description:
Canonical runtime context carried across request entry, agent execution, tool execution, and security-configuration mutation so that audit and policy decisions share one stable identity envelope. The minimum P0 payload includes trace_id, user_id, session_id, root_session_id, agent_id, channel, the acted-on security scope when present, and enough trusted provenance metadata to distinguish audited runtime continuity from replayed or cloned session usage.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

attributes.name: integrity_binding
attributes.description: Opaque provenance binding or signature reference that lets downstream guards verify the context originated from a trusted audited runtime rather than an injected or replayed process.

elements.name: Audit Event
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Event
elements.description:
Canonical append-only security audit record that captures event identity, event type, actor, session, agent, resource or configuration scope, decision or result metadata, prior_hash, current_hash, and sufficient fields to support integrity verification, remote integrity mirroring, replay detection, and basic operator query.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

attributes.name: sync_status
attributes.description: Per-entry uplink state for DEMO integrity mirroring. Valid values are PENDING, SYNCED, and FAILED.

attributes.name: sequence_number
attributes.description: Monotonic sequence ordinal used by the local verifier and cloud mirror to detect missing, duplicated, or replayed operations within a trusted trace or session.

attributes.name: confirmation_signature
attributes.description: Optional user confirmation signature persisted on audit entries that record explicit approval for high-risk actions.

attributes.name: auth_token_ref
attributes.description: Optional reference to the confirmation token or authentication artifact that authorized a high-risk action without embedding the raw secret in the audit payload.

attributes.name: resource_access_summary
attributes.description: Optional summary of resource category, access frequency, and aggregation scope used by cloud-side behavior analytics to distinguish normal work from silent exfiltration patterns.

elements.name: Audit Checkpoint Anchor
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Checkpoint Anchor
elements.description:
Restricted checkpoint record stored outside the primary audit-event log so tail truncation becomes detectable. The minimum P0 payload includes anchored_event_id, global_hash, checkpoint_reason, checkpoint_timestamp, and the protected storage location metadata used during startup integrity verification.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Tiered Audit Stream
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Tiered Audit Stream
elements.description:
Audit write orchestration that separates L1 critical events from L2 informational reasoning events. L1 uses synchronous durable writes and checkpoint updates before sensitive actions continue, while L2 batches asynchronously and remains operator-visible as pending verification until anchored.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: High Risk Tool Guard
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/High Risk Tool Guard
elements.description:
Interceptor that evaluates every tool invocation against the configured risk policy at the tool-call boundary. Whenever an agent attempts to invoke a tool marked with High risk level, it pauses execution, requires trusted Security Context provenance plus a user-provided confirmation token or signature, records the confirmation as a synchronous L1 USER_CONFIRMATION audit event, and only then releases the blocked tool execution path. Prompt content, nested instructions, or claimed test-mode narratives cannot bypass this enforcement.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Built-in Tool Rule Integrity
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/High Risk Tool Guard/Built-in Tool Rule Integrity
elements.description:
Out-of-band integrity verification for bundled dangerous shell command rules. Compares LF-normalized SHA-256 digests against a signed manifest, warns on semantic tamper without blocking rule loading in the first implementation, and supports trusted repair from a pinned official source.
#file:design/DesignDocByHuangsiyu/security_config_integrity_design.md
#file:src/qwenpaw/security/tool_guard/rules_integrity.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/security/tool_guard/rules/rules_manifest.json

attributes.name: hash_normalization_rule
attributes.value: SHA-256 over LF-normalized UTF-8 content; CRLF and lone CR drift must not false-positive when semantic rule content is unchanged

attributes.name: warn_only_policy
attributes.value: Integrity failures are logged and surfaced through the rules-integrity API and Security settings UI, but bundled rules continue loading until a future strict mode is introduced

elements.name: Audit Uplink Manager
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Manager
elements.description:
Manager responsible for asynchronously pushing the local ledger tail state, including last event id and global hash, plus L1 critical audit records to the cloud situational awareness platform. The DEMO path uses fire-and-forget with retries, supports resume after reconnect, and must perform a pre-upload handshake against the cloud mirror before sending new data.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Heartbeat Emitter
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Manager/Heartbeat Emitter
elements.description:
Emitter that periodically reads the most recent EventID and GlobalHash from the local audit ledger and sends them, together with client identity proof, to the Security Center as a lease heartbeat. The DEMO cadence is periodic, for example every five minutes.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Audit Uplink Service
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Service
elements.description:
Edge-side transport adapter that implements secure audit uplink. Whenever an L1 audit record or critical anchor is durably written, it triggers an asynchronous HTTPS or gRPC call that sends an encrypted audit summary toward the Security Center while preserving retry and resume semantics under intermittent connectivity.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Security Schema Contract
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Security Schema Contract
elements.description:
Shared contract that fixes the hash algorithm, canonical JSON field ordering, and serialization rules used by both edge-side uplink and cloud-side verification. Its purpose is to prevent false integrity failures caused by formatting drift rather than real tampering.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Security Center
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center
elements.description:
Security hub deployed in a controlled environment such as cloud or server infrastructure. It aggregates audit uplinks from multiple QwenPaw nodes, maintains per-client shadow audit chains, reconstructs end-to-end evidence chains for operator review, triggers real-time data continuity alerts whenever incoming integrity state diverges from the stored cloud expectation, blocks replayed session lineages, and surfaces abnormal behavior patterns such as covert low-risk data sweeps in a business-facing visual console.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Audit Lease Monitor
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Audit Lease Monitor
elements.description:
Monitor that maintains the lease registry for all connected clients. It applies a TTL threshold, for example thirty minutes, marks devices UNTRUSTED when no valid heartbeat is received within the threshold, and drives dashboard state plus forced-recovery requirements on reconnect.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

elements.name: Cloud Integrity Stub
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Cloud Integrity Stub
elements.description:
Logical verification stub inside the Security Center used for the DEMO. It receives critical audit anchors and L1 events from one QwenPaw client, maintains a shadow hash chain for that client, validates each uplink by comparing incoming prior_hash against the cloud-stored current_hash, and rejects replayed or cloned session activity when the presented trace lineage does not match the next expected sequence.
#file:design/prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope.md

attributes.name: external_scope
attributes.value: Cloud-side verifier modeled as an external application-side collaboration participant rather than a repository-owned runtime component.

elements.name: Security Event Ingestion V1
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Security Event Ingestion V1
elements.description:
Security Center V1 capability that receives security events from company internal systems over HTTP, validates sourceSystem, eventTypeId, schemaVersion, summary, occurredAt, and payload fields against repository configuration, durably persists legal events before returning success, stores rejected submissions as failure reception records, and exposes event list/detail consumption to security operators. V1 is intentionally an ingestion and viewing inbox, not an alerting, ticketing, notification, remediation, assignment, comment, export, statistics, authentication, or external third-party intake platform.
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-prd.md
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-decision-tree.md

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.value: sec-event-ingestion-v1

attributes.name: scope_rule
attributes.value: Only company internal systems may submit events in V1. V1 does not provide requester identity management or request authentication, so sourceSystem allow-listing, enabled flags, event-type authorization, and schema-version validation are mandatory data-quality controls.

attributes.name: persistence_rule
attributes.value: A legal security event must be durably persisted before the submit API returns success. If persistence fails, the API must return failure. Illegal events must not enter the business event list but must be retained as bounded failure reception records for traceability.

attributes.name: idempotency_rule
attributes.value: The idempotency key is sourceSystem plus eventId. A normalized identical repeat returns success without a new business event; the same key with a different eventTypeId, schemaVersion, or field values is rejected and recorded as an idempotency conflict; different sourceSystem values may reuse the same eventId.

attributes.name: web_inbox_rule
attributes.value: The operator web surface is a security event inbox. It defaults to receivedAt descending, supports time range, sourceSystem, event type, and severity filters, lists only configured list fields, opens stable detail URLs by sourceSystem plus eventId, and shows base facts, labeled structured payload, undefined fields, and read-only raw payload without allowing V1 configuration editing.

attributes.name: v1_non_goals
attributes.value: No requester authentication, third-party external intake, automatic alerting, ticket workflow, notification, automatic remediation, event status transition, owner assignment, comments, report export, statistics dashboard, Web self-service event configuration editing, historical schema display policy, or default retention promise for legal events and failure records.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-accept-legal-event
testcases.description: When a configured internal source submits a complete legal security event, Security Center must accept it only after durable persistence and make it visible to the operator event inbox.
testcases.Input: Control point: submit a legal HTTP event from an enabled configured sourceSystem with an allowed eventTypeId, valid schemaVersion, required summary, occurredAt, severity, and payload fields; then immediately query the event list and detail surfaces.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: the API returns success with eventId and duplicate=false only after persistence, the event appears in the Web/API list ordered by receivedAt, detail lookup by sourceSystem plus eventId succeeds, and receivedAt is generated by the backend rather than trusted from the request.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-reject-invalid-config-boundary
testcases.description: Unknown or disabled source systems, unauthorized event types, unknown event types, unknown schema versions, and invalid payload fields must be rejected without entering the business event list.
testcases.Input: Control point: submit events that violate sourceSystem allow-listing or enabled status, source-to-eventType authorization, eventTypeId existence, schemaVersion existence, required payload fields, field types, or enum values.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: each request returns a clear failure reason, no rejected event appears in the business event list, and a failure reception record exists with received time, submitted source field, submitted event type field, failure reason, and bounded request summary.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-preserve-undefined-payload-fields
testcases.description: Configured payload fields define the primary structured event contract, while extra payload fields remain preserved but separated as undefined fields.
testcases.Input: Control point: submit a legal event whose payload includes all required configured fields plus at least one field not declared by the schema configuration.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: the event is saved successfully, configured list columns exclude the undefined field, the detail page/API shows configured fields with labels, and the undefined payload field appears only in the undefined-fields detail area and raw payload view.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-enforce-idempotency
testcases.description: The same sourceSystem plus eventId must represent one event fact and cannot be reused for conflicting event content.
testcases.Input: Control point: submit the same normalized event twice, then submit the same sourceSystem plus eventId with a different eventTypeId, schemaVersion, or payload field value, and separately submit the same eventId from a different sourceSystem.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: identical repeat returns success with duplicate=true and no second business event; conflicting same-key submissions are rejected and recorded as idempotency conflicts; different sourceSystem plus same eventId is accepted as a distinct event.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-render-web-list-and-detail
testcases.description: Security operators must be able to use the Web inbox list and stable detail page to inspect accepted security events.
testcases.Input: Control point: seed multiple accepted events with different receivedAt, occurredAt, sourceSystem, eventTypeId, severity, configured list payload fields, undefined fields, and raw payloads; open the Web list, apply time/source/type/severity filters, and navigate to a detail URL.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: the list defaults to receivedAt descending, shows receivedAt, occurredAt, sourceSystem, event type display name, severity, summary, eventId, and configured list payload fields; filters constrain visible rows; detail URL reopens the same event and displays base facts, labeled structured payload, undefined fields, and a read-only raw payload without rendering very large payloads unbounded.

testcases.name: sec-event-ingestion-v1-bound-failure-records
testcases.description: Illegal or internally failed submissions must remain traceable without polluting business events or allowing oversized payload storage to degrade the system.
testcases.Input: Control point: submit illegal events for disallowed source, disallowed type, schema failure, payload validation failure, idempotency conflict, simulated persistence error, and an oversized illegal payload.
testcases.TestResults: Observation point: business event list remains clean for illegal submissions, failure records are queryable with received time, source field, event type field, failure reason, and bounded request summary, database write failure returns failure instead of success, and oversized illegal payload handling stores only a bounded summary or rejects safely without storage/page instability.

elements.name: Security Event Contract Config
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Security Event Ingestion V1/Security Event Contract Config
elements.description:
Configuration data that defines which internal source systems are enabled, which event types each source may submit, event type display metadata, schema versions, payload field names, labels, types, required flags, enum values, maximum lengths, and list-display flags. It defines event contracts and Web display metadata only, not business workflow logic.
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-prd.md

elements.name: Security Event
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Security Event Ingestion V1/Security Event
elements.description:
Accepted security event fact persisted by Security Center. Its identity is sourceSystem plus eventId, where eventId is a single event-instance id rather than a series or aggregate id. It stores eventTypeId, schemaVersion, severity, caller-provided summary, caller-provided occurredAt, backend-generated receivedAt, structured payload, undefined payload fields, and raw payload for operator detail review.
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-prd.md

elements.name: Security Event Failure Record
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Security Event Ingestion V1/Security Event Failure Record
elements.description:
Trace record for rejected or failed event submissions. It captures received time, submitted source field, submitted event type field, failure reason, and bounded request summary for source rejection, type rejection, schema rejection, payload validation failure, idempotency conflict, persistence failure, and oversized invalid payload handling without inserting an illegal business event.
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-prd.md

elements.name: Security Event Inbox Web
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Security Event Ingestion V1/Security Event Inbox Web
elements.description:
Operator-facing Web inbox for accepted security events. It provides a receivedAt-descending list with core columns and configured list payload fields, supports time range, sourceSystem, eventTypeId, and severity filters, and provides stable detail URLs that show base facts, labeled structured payload, undefined fields, and read-only raw payload with large-payload protection.
#file:design/prd/security-event-ingestion-prd.md

relationships.statement: Backend Runtime is part of QwenPaw Platform.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation is part of Backend Runtime.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Security Schema Contract is part of Backend Runtime.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation standardizes the Security Context carried across trust-sensitive runtime boundaries.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation relies on the Implicit Security Context Manager to preserve identity across async and message-dispatch boundaries.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation persists and verifies Audit Event records as its canonical tamper-evident ledger payload.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation anchors audit continuity through the Audit Checkpoint Anchor so tail truncation remains detectable after restart.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation uses the Tiered Audit Stream to separate synchronous critical durability from asynchronous informational batching.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation uses High Risk Tool Guard to block high-risk tool execution until explicit user confirmation is durably recorded.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: High Risk Tool Guard persists USER_CONFIRMATION audit events through the canonical Audit Event schema before releasing execution.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: High Risk Tool Guard owns Built-in Tool Rule Integrity as the out-of-band verification seam for bundled dangerous shell command rules.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Local Security Audit Foundation serves Audit Uplink Manager by exposing the local tamper-evident ledger and anchored tail state needed for uplink coordination.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Heartbeat Emitter serves Audit Uplink Manager by publishing periodic lease heartbeats derived from the local audit tail state.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Heartbeat Emitter reads the current Audit Event tail to publish EventID and GlobalHash as lease heartbeat state.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Audit Uplink Service serves Audit Uplink Manager by performing the secure transport toward the cloud-side security hub.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Audit Uplink Manager serves QwenPaw Platform by providing outward audit transparency and remote integrity mirroring status.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Heartbeat Emitter serves Security Center by periodically sending client identity, latest EventID, and GlobalHash as a lease heartbeat.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Audit Uplink Service serves Security Center by delivering encrypted L1 audit summaries and critical anchors from the edge node.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Security Center is part of the QwenPaw Platform's cloud-edge security collaboration model.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Audit Lease Monitor is part of Security Center as the component that tracks client TTL expiry and UNTRUSTED status.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Cloud Integrity Stub is part of Security Center as the per-client shadow-chain verifier used in the DEMO.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Security Event Ingestion V1 is part of Security Center as the V1 internal-system security event intake and operator inbox capability.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Security Event Ingestion V1 uses Security Event Contract Config to validate source systems, event types, schema versions, payload fields, and Web display metadata.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Security Event Ingestion V1 persists legal submissions as Security Event records before returning successful API responses.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Security Event Ingestion V1 records rejected submissions and persistence failures as Security Event Failure Record entries without adding them to the business event list.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Security Event Ingestion V1 serves Security Event Inbox Web with accepted event list, filter, detail, structured payload, undefined field, and raw payload data.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Security Event Inbox Web reads Security Event records to render the operator list and stable event detail pages.
relationships.name: Access

relationships.statement: Audit Lease Monitor coordinates with Cloud Integrity Stub to require full-gap validation before an UNTRUSTED client can recover trusted status.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Security Schema Contract fixes the canonical Audit Event hashing and serialization rules shared across edge and cloud verification.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Audit Uplink Service is associated with Security Schema Contract so transport payloads use the exact canonical hashing and field-order semantics expected by verification.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Security Center is associated with Security Schema Contract so cloud-side mirroring and alerting validate the same canonical payload semantics as the edge node.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Audit Uplink Manager associates with Cloud Integrity Stub over the cross-boundary uplink path used for hash alignment and critical-audit mirroring.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Local Operator CLI is part of QwenPaw Platform.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Console Frontend is part of QwenPaw Platform.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Docs Website is part of QwenPaw Platform.
relationships.name: Composition

relationships.statement: Architecture Validator Assets support QwenPaw Platform governance.
relationships.name: Association

relationships.statement: Backend Runtime serves Local Operator CLI through shared runtime services and helpers.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Backend Runtime serves Console Frontend through repository-owned APIs.
relationships.name: Serving

relationships.statement: Docs Website describes and publishes QwenPaw Platform capabilities.
relationships.name: Association

views.description: Stable platform slices and the strengthened P0 local security audit foundation required for safe local operation.

Intent view for the strengthened local security audit closure that standardizes implicit context propagation, audit event shape, checkpoint-anchored tamper evidence, out-of-band configuration consistency verification, tiered logging semantics, and edge-to-cloud integrity mirroring with pre-upload handshake.
Cross-boundary view showing how edge-side audit capture, uplink transport, shared canonical schema rules, and cloud-side mirroring combine into one security collaboration loop.

Intent view for the V1 Security Center event intake inbox: internal systems submit events over HTTP, backend validates against configuration, legal events persist before success, illegal submissions become failure records, and security operators consume list/detail Web views.
